Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Microstructural evolution and mechanical properties of differently heat-treated binder jet printed samples from gas- and water-atomized alloy 625 powders

Composition_Info.Note: Nominal composition from manufacturer (Carpenter Technology Corporation). GA powder oxygen content 0.04 wt%, carbon content 0.01 wt% (as-received by LECO analysis). Ni computed as balance. Nominal composition provided by supplier; Al, Ti not reported for GA by supplier.

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized alloy 625 powder (Carpenter Technology Corporation, N2 gas atomized) binder jet printed using M-Flex ExOne BJP printer. Green parts cured at 175°C in a JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace. Sintered in a Lindberg tube furnace in an alumina powder bed under vacuum with the following heating profile: 5°C/min from RT to 600°C, 3.2°C/min to 1000°C, 2.8°C/min to 1225°C, held for 4 h, cooled at 1°C/min to 1200°C, 3.1°C/min to 500°C, then cooled to RT.
Process_Text.simplified: BJP printed (M-Flex ExOne), cured at 175°C, vacuum sintered at 1225°C for 4 h in alumina powder bed (Lindberg tube furnace).

Process_Info.Equipment: M-Flex ExOne binder jet printer; JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace (curing); Lindberg tube furnace (sintering)

Microstructure_Text.original: GA samples sintered at 1225°C were porous and partially connected, indicating sub-optimal sintering temperature. Two pore types present: large pores at grain boundaries and small pores within grains. The latter probably originated from pores within powder particles unable to annihilate. Carbide precipitates detected at grain boundaries forming during cooling. Grain size 39 ± 4 μm by ImageJ; average pore size 41 ± 18 μm.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Partially sintered GA sample with large inter-particle and intra-grain pores; carbides at grain boundaries; equiaxed grains 39 μm average; relative density 85%.

Properties_Info.Note: HV0.1 scale; Leco LM 800 microhardness tester

Composition_Info.Note: Nominal from manufacturer (Carpenter Technology). GA powder O=0.04 wt%, C=0.01 wt% (LECO). Ni computed as balance.

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized alloy 625 powder binder jet printed using M-Flex ExOne BJP printer. Green parts cured at 175°C in JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace. Sintered in Lindberg tube furnace, alumina powder bed, vacuum: 5°C/min to 600°C, 3.2°C/min to 1000°C, 2.8°C/min to 1240°C, held 4 h, cooled 1°C/min to 1200°C, 3.1°C/min to 500°C, then RT.
Process_Text.simplified: BJP printed (M-Flex ExOne), cured at 175°C, vacuum sintered at 1240°C for 4 h.

Process_Info.Equipment: M-Flex ExOne binder jet printer; JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace (curing); Lindberg tube furnace (sintering)

Microstructure_Text.original: GA sample sintered at 1240°C showing progression from porous to denser microstructure. Grain size 45 ± 11 μm by ImageJ; pore size 36 ± 10 μm. Pores at grain boundaries and within grains.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: GA sintered at 1240°C; equiaxed grains ~45 μm; average pore size 36 μm.

Properties_Info.Note: HV0.1 scale; Leco LM 800 microhardness tester

Composition_Info.Note: Nominal from manufacturer (Carpenter Technology). GA powder O=0.04 wt%, C=0.01 wt% (LECO). Ni computed as balance.

Process_Info.Equipment: M-Flex ExOne binder jet printer; JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace; Lindberg tube furnace

Microstructure_Text.original: GA sample sintered at 1255°C with reduced porosity compared to 1225°C. Grain size 62 ± 20 μm; pore size 27 ± 13 μm.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: GA sintered at 1255°C; equiaxed grains ~62 μm; pore size 27 μm.

Properties_Info.Note: HV0.1 scale; Leco LM 800

Sintered in Lindberg tube furnace, alumina powder bed, vacuum: 5°C/min to 600°C, 3.2°C/min to 1000°C, 2.8°C/min to 1270°C, held 4 h, cooled 1°C/min to 1200°C, 3.1°C/min to 500°C, then RT.

Microstructure_Text.original: GA sample sintered at 1270°C showing equiaxed grains, most pores within grains disappeared. Grain size 69 ± 23 μm; average pore size 20 ± 4 μm. Relative density close to 95%.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: GA sintered at 1270°C; equiaxed grains ~69 μm; most internal pores eliminated; relative density ~95%.

Properties_Info.Note: HV0.1 scale; Leco LM 800

Composition_Info.Note: GA powder O=0.04 wt%, C=0.01 wt% (LECO as-received); after printing/curing: O=0.28 wt%, C=0.44 wt%. Ni computed as balance.

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized alloy 625 powder binder jet printed using M-Flex ExOne BJP printer. Green parts cured at 175°C in JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace. Sintered in Lindberg tube furnace, alumina powder bed, vacuum: 5°C/min to 600°C, 3.2°C/min to 1000°C, 2.8°C/min to 1285°C, held 4 h, cooled 1°C/min to 1200°C, 3.1°C/min to 500°C, then RT. Super-solidus liquid phase sintering active at ≥1283°C.
Process_Text.simplified: BJP printed, cured 175°C, vacuum sintered at 1285°C for 4 h; super-solidus liquid phase sintering active.

Process_Info.Equipment: M-Flex ExOne binder jet printer; JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace; Lindberg tube furnace

Microstructure_Text.original: GA sample sintered at 1285°C showing highest density (99.2%). Equiaxed grains with super-solidus liquid phase sintering active. Grain size 89 ± 21 μm; small average pore size 4.5 ± 1.2 μm. MC carbides and M6C at grain boundary triple points identified by EDS. Elemental segregation of Nb, Mo, Cr at grain boundaries. SEM with EDS confirms carbide composition (C, Cr, Mo, Nb, Ni). Mostly ductile fracture dimples in tensile fracture surface.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fully dense GA sample (99.2%) sintered at 1285°C; equiaxed grains 89 μm; small residual pores 4.5 μm; MC/M6C/M23C6 carbides at grain boundaries; primarily ductile fracture.

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized alloy 625 powder binder jet printed using M-Flex ExOne BJP printer. Cured at 175°C in JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace. Sintered in Lindberg tube furnace, alumina powder bed, vacuum: 5°C/min to 600°C, 3.2°C/min to 1000°C, 2.8°C/min to 1300°C, held 4 h, cooled 1°C/min to 1200°C, 3.1°C/min to 500°C, then RT. Super-solidus liquid phase sintering active. Consolidation and elemental segregation at grain boundaries observed.
Process_Text.simplified: BJP printed, cured 175°C, vacuum sintered at 1300°C for 4 h; liquid phase sintering; elemental segregation at grain boundaries.

Process_Info.Equipment: M-Flex ExOne binder jet printer; JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace; Lindberg tube furnace

Microstructure_Text.original: GA sample sintered at 1300°C showing consolidation and elemental segregation at grain boundaries due to liquid phase sintering. Grain size 106 ± 27 μm; pore size 2.6 ± 0.4 μm. EDS shows brighter areas at grain boundaries with higher concentrations of Mo, Nb, Ni, C suggesting MC carbides. Points 4 and 5 in Fig. 5b show MC/M23C6 carbides; point 6 suggests M6C at grain boundary triple points; point 3 shows matrix composition close to alloy 625.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: GA sintered at 1300°C; large equiaxed grains 106 μm; very small pores 2.6 μm; MC/M23C6/M6C carbides at grain boundaries; Nb+Mo elemental segregation.

Properties_Info.Note: HV0.1 scale; Leco LM 800; hardness decreased from 1285°C peak due to grain coarsening and elemental segregation

Composition_Info.Note: GA powder O=0.04 wt%, C=0.01 wt% (LECO as-received). Ni computed as balance.

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized alloy 625 powder binder jet printed using M-Flex ExOne BJP printer. Green parts cured at 175°C in JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace. Sintered in Lindberg tube furnace, alumina powder bed, vacuum at 1285°C for 4 h (with heating/cooling profile: 5°C/min to 600°C, 3.2°C/min to 1000°C, 2.8°C/min to 1285°C; cooling 1°C/min to 1200°C, 3.1°C/min to 500°C, then RT). After sintering, solution treated at 1150°C for 2 h and aged at 745°C for 20 h.
Process_Text.simplified: BJP printed, cured 175°C, vacuum sintered at 1285°C for 4 h, then solution treated at 1150°C/2 h and aged at 745°C/20 h.

Process_Info.Equipment: M-Flex ExOne binder jet printer; JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace; Lindberg tube furnace; MTS 880 (tensile)

Microstructure_Text.original: GA sample sintered at 1285°C and then solution treated at 1150°C/2 h followed by aging at 745°C/20 h. SEM shows light-grey precipitates within grains and at grain boundaries (Fig. 5c,d). EDS shows: matrix (point 7) near nominal alloy 625 composition; grain boundary precipitates (points 8,9) mainly Mo, Nb, C, 2.5-4.0 μm, identified as MC carbides; intragranular precipitates (points 10,11) of C, Nb, Mo, Cr, Ni, 0.5-1.0 μm, identified as MC and M23C6. Oxygen increased in carbide precipitates (oxidation of MC/M23C6). Fracture surface shows dimples, particle facets of carbide precipitates, and some micro-cracks; mixed mode fracture (intergranular + ductile dimple).
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Aged GA sample (1285°C sinter + 1150°C ST + 745°C/20 h age); fine carbide precipitates at grain boundaries (MC, 2.5-4 μm) and within grains (MC/M23C6, 0.5-1 μm); mixed mode fracture.

Nominal includes upper limits: Al<0.4, Ti<0.4, Co<1.0 wt%; Ni computed as balance from provided values. Measured Ni computed as balance from EDS sum.

Process_Text.original: Water-atomized alloy 625 powder (HAI Advanced Material Specialists, Inc.) binder jet printed using M-Flex ExOne BJP printer. Green parts cured at 175°C in JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace. Sintered in Lindberg tube furnace, alumina powder bed, vacuum: 5°C/min to 600°C, 3.2°C/min to 1000°C, 2.8°C/min to 1225°C, held 4 h, cooled 1°C/min to 1200°C, 3.1°C/min to 500°C, then RT.
Process_Text.simplified: BJP printed (M-Flex ExOne), cured 175°C, vacuum sintered at 1225°C for 4 h.

Process_Info.Equipment: M-Flex ExOne binder jet printer; JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace; Lindberg tube furnace

Microstructure_Text.original: WA sample sintered at 1225°C showing a large number of pores. Bright precipitates at grain boundaries identified as carbides (formation of liquid phase during sintering). Large pores at grain boundaries and smaller pores within grains. High oxygen content (1.90 wt%) promotes surface oxide formation at prior particle boundaries, inhibiting densification. Grain size 80 ± 25 μm; pore size 20 ± 7 μm.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: WA sintered at 1225°C; porous microstructure; carbide and oxide precipitates at grain boundaries; grain size 80 μm; pore size 20 μm.

Properties_Info.Note: HV0.1 scale; Leco LM 800

Composition_Info.Note: WA powder O=1.90 wt%, C=0.09 wt% (LECO as-received). Ni in nominal computed as balance. Upper limits in nominal: Al<0.4, Ti<0.4, Co<1.0 wt%.

Microstructure_Text.simplified: WA sintered at 1240°C; porous; carbide/oxide at grain boundaries; grain size 83 μm; pore size 20 μm.

Super-solidus liquid phase sintering active at ≥1255°C for WA (solidus 1253°C).

Microstructure_Text.original: WA sample sintered at 1255°C with super-solidus liquid phase sintering active. Alloying element segregation at grain boundaries increased. Large pores remain. Carbides and oxides increased at grain boundaries. Grain size 86 ± 25 μm; pore size 20 ± 7 μm.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: WA sintered at 1255°C with liquid phase sintering; grain boundary segregation; large pores; carbide/oxide increase; grain size 86 μm.

Properties_Info.Note: HV0.1 scale; Leco LM 800

Process_Text.original: Water-atomized alloy 625 powder binder jet printed using M-Flex ExOne BJP printer. Cured at 175°C in JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace. Sintered in Lindberg tube furnace, alumina powder bed, vacuum: 5°C/min to 600°C, 3.2°C/min to 1000°C, 2.8°C/min to 1270°C, held 4 h, cooled 1°C/min to 1200°C, 3.1°C/min to 500°C, then RT. Maximum density for WA samples at this temperature (95%).
Process_Text.simplified: BJP printed, cured 175°C, vacuum sintered at 1270°C for 4 h; maximum density state for WA (95%).

Process_Info.Equipment: M-Flex ExOne binder jet printer; JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace; Lindberg tube furnace; MTS 880 (tensile)

Microstructure_Text.original: WA sample sintered at 1270°C reaching maximum relative density of 95%. Some pore consolidation but large pores remain. Carbides and oxides at grain boundaries, forming MC type and film-like MC/M6C. Cr, Nb, Mo depleted zones around grain boundaries. High oxygen content leads to surface oxides at prior particle boundaries inhibiting densification. Grain size 88 ± 26 μm; pore size 21 ± 9 μm. Fracture surface shows small precipitates and micro-cracks; mixed brittle-ductile failure.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: WA at maximum density (95%); grain size 88 μm; large pores remain; extensive carbide/oxide at grain boundaries; Cr/Nb/Mo depletion zones; mixed brittle-ductile fracture.

Microstructure_Text.simplified: WA at 1285°C; density below 1270°C maximum; surface melting with alumina adhesion; grain size 91 μm; pore size 22 μm.

Process_Text.original: Water-atomized alloy 625 powder binder jet printed using M-Flex ExOne BJP printer. Cured at 175°C in JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace. Sintered in Lindberg tube furnace, alumina powder bed, vacuum: 5°C/min to 600°C, 3.2°C/min to 1000°C, 2.8°C/min to 1300°C, held 4 h, cooled 1°C/min to 1200°C, 3.1°C/min to 500°C, then RT. Pores still present; precipitates increased along grain boundaries.
Process_Text.simplified: BJP printed, cured 175°C, vacuum sintered at 1300°C for 4 h; pores persist; increased carbide/oxide precipitates.

Process_Info.Equipment: M-Flex ExOne binder jet printer; JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace; Lindberg tube furnace

Microstructure_Text.original: WA sample sintered at 1300°C with pores still present and precipitates increased along grain boundaries. Bright areas (point 2, Fig. 5a) identified by EDS as mainly Nb, Mo, C (MC carbide). High oxygen and carbon at grain boundaries; surface oxides and preferential precipitation at prior particle boundaries inhibit densification. Solid-state diffusion and grain boundary migration hindered by surface oxides. Film-like MC and M6C carbide formation at grain boundaries. Grain size 94 ± 27 μm; pore size 23 ± 10 μm.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: WA at 1300°C; pores persist; MC/M6C carbides abundant at grain boundaries; film-like carbide formation; grain size 94 μm; oxide-inhibited densification.

Properties_Info.Note: HV0.1 scale; Leco LM 800

Composition_Info.Note: WA powder O=1.90 wt%, C=0.09 wt% (LECO as-received). Ni computed as balance. Upper limits: Al<0.4, Ti<0.4, Co<1.0 wt%.

Process_Text.original: Water-atomized alloy 625 powder binder jet printed using M-Flex ExOne BJP printer. Cured at 175°C in JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace. Sintered in Lindberg tube furnace, alumina powder bed, vacuum at 1270°C for 4 h (heating/cooling profile same as other samples). After sintering, solution treated at 1150°C for 2 h and aged at 745°C for 20 h.
Process_Text.simplified: BJP printed, cured 175°C, vacuum sintered at 1270°C for 4 h, then solution treated at 1150°C/2 h and aged at 745°C/20 h.

Process_Info.Equipment: M-Flex ExOne binder jet printer; JPW Design & Manufacturing furnace; Lindberg tube furnace; MTS 880 (tensile)

Microstructure_Text.original: WA sample sintered at 1270°C then solution treated at 1150°C/2 h and aged at 745°C/20 h. SEM shows various precipitates (Fig. 5e): (1) white precipitates at grain boundaries (point 14) composed of Ni, Mo, Cr, C (MC-type, strong carbide formers up to 87.6%); (2) oval-shaped precipitates at grain boundary (area 12) with Ni, Mo, Cr indicating M6C/MC/M23C6 or Laves; (3) intragranular precipitates (area 13) with Ni, Mo, Cr suggesting M6C/MC/M23C6 or Laves. Large amount of carbides and oxides at grain boundaries with Cr, Nb, Mo depleted zones. Fracture surface shows more precipitates (carbides and possibly intermetallic phases) as crack initiation sites.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Aged WA sample; large carbide/oxide at grain boundaries; Cr/Nb/Mo depletion zones; MC/M6C/M23C6/Laves precipitates within grains and at grain boundaries; more brittle fracture character.
